Iran conflict disrupts global shipping as tankers are stranded and damaged
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed; threatens to fire on ships attempting
Three tankers damaged in the Gulf amid U.S.-Iran conflict escalation
Major container lines pause/divert Red Sea and Suez transits after strikes on Ir
Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to record levels
Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike (source)
Qatar LNG output disrupted amid regional strikes

Iraqi Kurdistan oil output disrupted amid wider regional strikes

edges.label: retaliation/escalation
edges.why: Hormuz closure threat is described as part of the escalation following the initial strikes.

edges.label: kinetic spillover to maritime traffic
edges.why: A declared closure/threat environment increases likelihood of attacks/incidents against tankers operating near the chokepoint.

edges.label: risk repricing
edges.why: Physical damage events are a direct trigger for insurers raising rates or withdrawing cover.

edges.label: cost transmission
edges.why: Reduced cover/greater war-risk premia increase total voyage costs and constrain available tonnage.

edges.label: supply-chokepoint risk premium
edges.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for oil and LNG; closure threats typically lift crude risk premium.

edges.label: inflation + funding stress
edges.why: Higher delivered energy/freight costs can lift inflation expectations and widen credit spreads on shock risk.

edges.label: macro shock
edges.why: Energy price spikes and supply uncertainty historically trigger broad risk-off positioning.

edges.label: risk transmission
edges.why: Risk-off conditions often weaken EM FX, especially for energy importers facing worse terms of trade.
